checks.a_analysis: The output faithfully represents the paragraphs and headings found in the source document. The resolution title, WHEREAS clauses, and concluding statement all have a direct basis in the original text. The table provided at the end (reading order 12) is a structured representation of the signature blocks at the bottom of the document, mapping the printed names and titles to fields; this is an acceptable structural reformatting for data extraction and accessibility rather than fabrication of content.
The source document does not contain any phone numbers, email addresses, or URLs. The JSON output correctly reflects this absence and does not introduce any hallucinated contact details.

All sections of the single-page document are present in the output, including the header labels, the main resolution title, all five WHEREAS clauses, the resolving paragraph, the date of adoption, the signatures (reformatted as a table), and the county seal (represented as an image).

The original document contains two signature blocks rather than a formal table. The conversion process has correctly extracted the information from both blocks into a three-row table (one header and two data rows for each signer). This accurately captures all available signature data.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: While there are a few minor word omissions (noted in C6), no substantial portion of any section has been dropped. Each paragraph starts and ends correctly relative to the source, and the total amount of text lost per section is well below the 25% threshold for a major defect.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis:
There are three minor textual discrepancies where words or suffixes were omitted during the conversion/OCR process: 
1. In the first WHEREAS clause (reading_order 4), the title 'Rep' is missing from 'US Rep Norma T. Torres'.
2. In the fourth WHEREAS clause (reading_order 7), the verb 'are' is missing from the phrase '911 dispatchers are working'.
3. In the final resolving paragraph (reading_order 10), 'Protective Services Occupations' is incorrectly rendered as 'Protective Service Occupations', omitting the 's'.
While minor, these changes slightly alter the formal names and titles within the document.
checks.b_evidence_source: WHEREAS, in September of 2021, US Rep Norma T. Torres... WHEREAS, 911 dispatchers are working with... to “Protective Services Occupations”
checks.b_evidence_output: WHEREAS, in September of 2021, US Norma T. Torres... WHEREAS, 911 dispatchers working with... to “Protective Service Occupations”

checks.a_analysis: The conversion faithfully represents the text content without creating any extraneous duplicates. While some phrases are repeated (e.g., the description of the reclassification from 'Office and Administrative Support' to 'Protective Services Occupations'), this repetition is present in the source resolution text itself (first 'WHEREAS' clause and the final 'RESOLVED' clause). The signature names appearing in the metadata table at the end are part of a structured extraction of the signature block and do not constitute a duplication defect.

The heading hierarchy is logical and follows a standard pattern. The main document title is correctly assigned as an H1 (reading_order 3). The secondary bolded statement 'NOW THEREFORE BE IT RESOLVED...' is assigned as an H2 (reading_order 9). This follows a proper descending order (H1 to H2) without skipping levels, which is appropriate for accessibility and structural fidelity.

There are no tables in the source document. The table found in the output (reading_order 12) is a metadata table generated to capture the information in the signature blocks (Clerk and Chairman). This is a common structured data extraction and does not represent a corruption of a source table.

The reading order of the text elements follows the top-to-bottom, left-to-right flow of the source document perfectly. The placement of the 'NORTH CAROLINA' and 'MITCHELL COUNTY' headers at the beginning, followed by the title and clauses, matches the source. The Mitchell County seal image (reading_order 13) has been moved after the text content, which is a standard accessibility practice to allow screen readers to reach core text content first, and is listed as an acceptable change.
There are no formal lists (bulleted or numbered) in the source document. The various clauses are correctly identified as standard paragraphs.
There are no links in the source document, and none have been incorrectly injected into the output.

checks.a_analysis: The visual organization of the document is preserved in the HTML rendering. The header elements (North Carolina, Mitchell County), title, and resolution clauses are presented in a logical single-column layout that follows the source document. While fonts and exact spacing differ as expected in HTML, the document structure is intuitive and the information is found in the expected sequence.

The source document does not contain any tables. The HTML output introduces a 'Signatures' table to organize metadata about the signees, which is rendered clearly with borders and distinct headers. This is a standard conversion technique for accessibility and not a defect.

All meaningful images from the source PDF are present in the HTML output. This includes the Mitchell County seal and the handwritten signatures of Taylor McCurry and Steve Pitman, which have been extracted and rendered as images below the main text.

The HTML output contains significant visual duplication of the body content. Following the accessible HTML text and the individual signature images, the document renders several large images that are high-contrast/thresholded segments of the original page. These images repeat the title, all 'WHEREAS' clauses, the 'NOW THEREFORE' clause, and the signatures that were already presented in the main HTML body.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source document presents the resolution text once on page 1.
checks.b_evidence_output: The entire text of the resolution (e.g., 'RESOLUTION SUPPORTING THE RECLASSIFICATION...', 'WHEREAS, in September of 2021...', etc.) is rendered as HTML text at the top and then repeated as a series of white-on-black image blocks at the bottom of the page.
checks.b_evidence_location: Bottom of the HTML screenshot, following the signature images.
